name: Medellin Town Museum
address: 4XH6+QMH, Daan Bantayan Road, Medellin, Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Tala Pinoy; rating=4; time=2024-01-07T08:55:29.377755Z; relative=2 years ago; text=This Medellin ancestral home contains lots of the 1900s local history and heartache through multiple generations that lived and thrived here. If you look closely, you’ll see the picture of the late Medellin’s ex-mayor, Ricardo “Ricky” Ramirez who been assassinated in 2016. Now the old ancestral home contains all wooden tools such as sugarcane press, planter’s chair, memorabilia, stone corn grinder, amazing tugas (Philippine hardwood) floor and support beams, capiz panels, pedal assisted singer sewing machine, antique musical instruments, small bangka, etc just so many daily things that formed picture of how’s one lived during this period. Take your time and pondering some interesting history behind each item, or Google search for the function of these early 1900s items… you’ll pleasantly surprise as much as we have! || author=emil cimafranca; rating=5; time=2023-12-10T14:05:27.109457Z; relative=2 years ago; text=Learn about Medellin and appreciate how they preserve, maintain and educate tourists be it local and international. See the past life of our ancestors Even preserved a very amazing treasure and artifact from WW2 tanker. 👍 || author=peachy miranda; rating=5; time=2018-03-29T13:17:41.815Z; relative=8 years ago; text=MUSEO DE MEDELLIN

Built in the early 1900s, the Museo de Medellin, formerly known as the Tumulak Ancestral House was the home of the late mayor Victor Tumulak. During Mayor Tumulak’s time, this building was the place for gathering, for its location is very strategic.  It is placed at the heart of the municipality, across the Parish Church and near the sports complex.

In the 1960s, the same building accommodated the first high school in the municipality- Holy Name School.  Years later, the high school was transferred to its newly constructed building. Thereafter, the building was abandoned for years.

2007, the building was renovated. The ground floor of the building became a museum while the second floor, housed the community based training center for high speed sewing.

Now, the lot and the building which houses the museum is  owned by former mayor Manreza P. Lim. By joint agreement with the municipality of Medellin and Mayor Lim’s family, the building was refurbished, beautified and transformed into a museum.

The ground floor of the museum highlights the traditional carabao drawn sugar mill or “intosan”, some religious artifacts, traditional banca and vintage musical instruments. The second floor, called as the “lifestyle museum” features, wooden furnitures and brasswares. It also gives us a glimpse of the house interiors from way back.

Museo de Medellin was formally launched last June 12,2017. It is open Mondays to Fridays from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM.

-S.M.Embalzado || author=Miguel M.; rating=5; time=2020-12-22T10:27:05.161298Z; relative=5 years ago; text=Though the building is a little old, it's still holds artifacts that we're once used by our great grandfather and grandmothers. || author=sike waller; rating=2; time=2019-03-08T21:54:35.914640Z; relative=7 years ago; text=The place clearly needs upkeep.. So sad it doesn't have much to show about medellin's history...

description: Massive museum with collections of Filipino fine art, archaeology, botany, natural history & more.
sample_reviews:
author=Hobart Ferry; rating=5; time=2026-03-10T09:55:15.982702563Z; relative=a month ago; text=We visited the National Museum of Fine Arts and it was really impressive. The place is huge and full of amazing paintings and sculptures by Filipino artists. One of the highlights for me was seeing the famous Spoliarium in person—it’s even more incredible up close. The museum is clean, well organized, and the best part is the entrance is free. Definitely worth visiting if you want to learn more about Filipino art and history. 🎨 || author=Grayson’s Footprint; rating=5; time=2026-02-28T23:48:25.524832446Z; relative=a month ago; text=National Museum of Fine Arts is an impressive and spacious museum that houses a wide collection of Filipino artworks, from iconic masterpieces to modern and contemporary pieces, spread across multiple large floors that can easily take hours to explore. Best of all, admission is totally free, making it very welcoming for everyone. The building is well-maintained and thoughtfully designed with ramps, elevators, and wide walkways, making it highly accessible for seniors and PWDs. It’s peaceful, educational, and a must-visit for anyone who wants to experience Philippine art and culture without spending a peso. || author=monica starke; rating=5; time=2026-02-27T08:50:23.167836752Z; relative=a month ago; text=the National Museum of Fine Arts is a must  visit when in Manila. It’s a good place  to admire art, history, and beautiful architecture.

The building is stunning, with grand halls, high ceilings, and elegant details that make the space feel impressive. The galleries are thoughtfully curated and showcase an incredible collection of Filipino masterpieces.

The museum is well-organized and easy to navigate, with plenty of space to move around comfortably. It’s also free to enter, which makes it an accessible and enriching experience for both locals and visitors.  When we were visiting, there was a long line waiting to enter. We found out that as seniors over 65 we were able to bypass the line, So there was no wait time for us.  The museum seemed to be a popular spot for young people to visit.

Overall, the National Museum of Fine Arts offers a meaningful look into Philippine culture and artistic heritage. It’s a peaceful, inspiring place to spend a few hours while learning more about the country’s history through art. || author=Miguel; rating=5; time=2026-01-07T09:07:06.987641356Z; relative=3 months ago; text=A beautiful experience with free entrance for everyone. Seeing the Spoliarium in person is worth the trip alone. Since the building is huge and filled with so many galleries, you should allocate at least 2 to 3 hours to see it all.
It is best to bring a valid ID for registration and avoid bringing large bags as you will need to leave them at the baggage counter. Once you are finished here, you can easily walk over to the Museum of Natural History nearby to see the 'Tree of Life' structure. || author=Belle; rating=5; time=2025-12-25T07:24:33.881783560Z; relative=4 months ago; text=I visit the National Museum of Fine Arts at least once a year as a way to unwind and reconnect with our history. Introducing more guided tours daily across the National Museums would greatly enhance the experience, as it would help visitors better appreciate and understand the historical and artistic significance of the collections. PS. Spolarium painting was not open to the public when we visited this December 2025.

author=Frankie Cacafranca; rating=5; time=2026-01-31T06:07:06.024925785Z; relative=2 months ago; text=• Layout: Perfect size—everything is within easy walking distance.
• Rides: Excellent quality and very well-maintained.

• Suggestion: Wish there was free water; adding a few drinking fountains would be a huge plus for customers.

But my family of 14 enjoyed ourselves. Almost no wait in lines gave a very enjoyable time my all ages of my family. Arcades were fun, food was good. I am disappointed though that the ticket counter would not accept me as a senior. Im 65 years old, American but that should not matter. Staff was pleasant and friendly. || author=Papa Jan's Pasta; rating=5; time=2026-01-06T06:06:02.332216483Z; relative=3 months ago; text=You should definitely visit this park, especially during Christmas time, and especially for Snow World. Honestly, where else can you feel winter vibes while wearing a T-shirt? It’s the perfect holiday idea—cool snow, Christmas mood, and zero need to pack a jacket from overseas. ❄️😂

Most people seem to arrive in the late afternoon, which is actually great news if you come earlier. During lunchtime the park is calm, peaceful, and almost feels like you rented the place for yourself. You can enjoy the rides without long lines, no waiting, no stress—just walk, ride, repeat like a VIP.

The roller coaster is an absolute must. Even if you say “I’ll just watch,” you won’t. You’ll ride it… and probably want to ride it again. Meanwhile, Anjo Eye is perfect for families with small kids—smooth, fun, and just the right amount of excitement without giving parents a heart attack. 🎡😆

Overall, it’s a great mix of fun, relaxation, and Christmas magic. Come early, enjoy everything with ease, and leave just when the crowds start coming in. Big thanks to the park for the laughs, the memories, and the unexpected snow in the tropics. 🎅✨ || author=Irish; rating=5; time=2025-09-25T06:46:34.626947442Z; relative=7 months ago; text=We came at a bit of bad timing since it was raining and the outdoor rides were closed. We chose the taxi bump car, drop tower, and roller coaster, and overall still had a great time. || author=Willow Blair; rating=5; time=2026-02-27T05:16:06.830157005Z; relative=a month ago; text=We had so much fun at Anjo World Theme Park. Seeing the kids smile and have fun made the visit even more special. Definitely a wonderful spot for a family day out full of laughter and excitement.

name: Snow World Cebu
address: 6RX2+W2C, Minglanilla, Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Irish; rating=4; time=2025-09-25T06:58:26.630800132Z; relative=7 months ago; text=I must say, it’s really on the expensive side. At first, we only paid an extra ₱200 because we availed the 3-ride ticket. But if you want to try their 6-ft ice slide, you’ll have to pay an additional ₱150 each — though this already includes gloves and cute earmuffs in pink or sky blue. They also provide jackets for free, and you just need to make sure you’re wearing shoes. If not, you can buy/rent shoes from the counter, im not so sure.

Inside was fun but quite small. They sell hot chocolate for ₱60, and personal photos aren’t allowed. Instead, they have an official photographer and you’ll need to pay ₱200 for each of your photo if you want. (this includes both soft and hard copies).

I understand why it’s a bit pricey — maintaining the ice must cost a lot — but at least you get to experience what it feels like to be in snow, almost like being outside the country. || author=Michael Bacaltos; rating=3; time=2025-12-29T09:33:33.401494792Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Taking videos and photos with your mobile phones are not allowed.  Taking sneak shots only. || author=J Christopher Kus; rating=4; time=2023-12-13T20:38:18.018177Z; relative=2 years ago; text=Tucked into the back of a small strip-mall about an hours drive from Downtown Cebu is this quaint little amusement park called Anjo World.  Within this park is Snow World.

Now, I come from the Midwest, and I know cold and snow, but to a Filippina, this is an exotic world!

They provide coats (and, for an additional fee, gloves, earmuffs, and shoes, if the ones you wear would be slippery in snow).

They provide an adjustment period in the front, where the temperature is about 40F or 2C, so that your sweat evaporates and the Filippinos don't get hypothermia. Then they let you inside to their "winter wonderland" and walk around the two stories, before shooting down their ice-slide. Picture-taking is not allowed, and they have staff everywhere to enforce it.  Instead, they will gladly take pictures of you, then sell the prints at the end for 200 PHP each.

To a westerner, it is pretty lame.  For your Filippina partner, it is utterly fascinating.  I recommend it for a date!

You pay an entrance fee to Anjo and an additional fee for Snow World for a total of ~1100 PHP per person.  They are open Wednesday through Saturday I think. || author=Cherrylane Gonzales; rating=5; time=2019-06-13T14:44:49.443053Z; relative=6 years ago; text=Fun place to be with kids... Just needs more food shops.. would be much more fun if all rides will be available. Most major rides ( 🎡, 🎢) are still under construction. My kidseven my parents had so much fun with the bump cars... || author=Carlo Andrew Olano; rating=5; time=2018-11-12T01:10:25.505955935Z; relative=7 years ago; text=Snow world Cebu lets Cebuanos and visitors experience winter temperatures all year round. The 2000 square meter facility is the biggest snow theme park in the country. The entrance fee is 350 and they provide jackets and gloves but wearing extra layers of clothing would be suitable. They won’t allow you to bring phones and cameras inside but they have their in house photographers to do the job.  Their will be a coffee shop soon inside where people can enjoy coffee and other hot beverages.

sample_reviews:
author=Andi Ng; rating=4; time=2025-12-04T05:06:22.005915314Z; relative=4 months ago; text=We visited Sachiko’s Little Kyoto and the experience was overall enjoyable. The entrance fee is ₱120 per person, which is reasonable for the ambiance and photo spots inside.

The main challenge is the road going there. The path is narrow, with steep sides and several broken or rough sections. It’s not advisable to visit during or after heavy rain because the road can get unsafe. I truly hope they can allocate funds and find a way to improve the road to make it safer for tourists and for everyone who passes through the area.

Once you arrive, the place itself is charming and peaceful. They also offer affordable and authentic ramen, which was a pleasant surprise. The area has a relaxing vibe, beautiful photo spots, and a nice Japanese-inspired atmosphere. Despite the difficult road, we still enjoyed our visit.

Overall, it’s worth a stop if you're looking for a unique and budget-friendly experience—just be prepared for the challenging drive and hopefully the road gets fixed soon. || author=Kristian Yves Lovendino; rating=5; time=2026-01-27T16:58:37.098595457Z; relative=3 months ago; text=I love this place! The crew are all polite and helpful! I love that they hire interns from local schools. The Japanese food is SUPERB! Like super masarap ramen!   Driving there can be a little treacherous coz of the narrow rough roads. But it’s worth the drive!!!! name: WaterWorld Cebu
types: water_park, resort_hotel, hotel, amusement_park, lodging, point_of_interest, establishment
address: 8XJ7+37Q, Unnamed Road, Mandaue, Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Matas Štelmokaitis; rating=1; time=2026-03-05T03:06:59.629792081Z; relative=a month ago; text=I would not recommend this place. The big slides were not working and the staff told us they were unsafe to use. That immediately made me wonder about the safety of the entire park. If the main attractions are unsafe, why are they even open?

The children's pool also felt unsafe. There were areas that looked poorly maintained and it didn’t feel like a place where kids could play safely. On top of that, everywhere you look there is rust on the equipment and structures. It gives the impression that maintenance is not a priority.

For a water park, cleanliness and safety should be the most important things, but unfortunately that was not the experience we had. The park needs serious maintenance, repairs, and better safety standards before it should be charging visitors.

Very disappointing visit. 👎 || author=Neek Mercader; rating=5; time=2026-02-14T10:22:10.449801918Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We absolutely love the hotel for the amenities, the food, and the awesome rooms. Currently, the water park leaves a lot to be be desired, and a lot of fixing, with a lot of the water amenities not working. But service is a marked improvement from before when they used to be draconian with the rules, not allowing food, water (!) and other things to be brought inside, which now seems to have been relaxed. And although the water park itself may take out a few marks, the hotel more than compensates for it that it still deserves a five out of five. || author=Clint Divino; rating=1; time=2026-02-26T11:06:53.651425417Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Mostly a bad experience. Most of the intact or operating pool facilities can only be enjoyed by children. Cleanliness is also a major concern, as the pools are not tiled and are only covered by plaster, and some areas already have chipped and flaked, which leads to dirt and at times foul odor despite the chlorinated pool. The showers are also too few to be used by a lot of people at the same time.

The only decent experience was the food, but ordering them is a hassle since they do not take cash but instead require digital wrist bands that need to be topped up, and the station for topping up isn't located at the same place as the ordering station, so walking back and forth is a hassle. || author=MOVE; rating=5; time=2026-01-27T03:47:35.998560448Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Our stay at Waterworld Cebu was spectacular.

Conveniently located right next to Waterworld Park, which is open from Friday through Sunday only, the water park features a variety of thrilling water slides and is tons of fun for friends, couples, and families with kids.

The hotel itself was very impressive, with a massive, spacious entrance. The Waterworld Park entrance is situated right next to the concierge and elevators, and there's also a restaurant called Felicito’s on the ground level, along with an outdoor pool.

The rooms are among the largest I've encountered in Cebu—exceptionally clean and modern. The beds were comfy, and there's a big-screen TV (though the WiFi occasionally lags, disrupting streaming services; however, it worked reliably on our phones). Amenities include a fridge, spacious closet, separate toilet and shower, a large mirror with lighting, a couch, and excellent room lighting. A rug is thoughtfully placed in front of the bed.

We enjoyed a nice view of the water park from our room. The hot water was reliable and one of the best we've experienced in Cebu hotels—it never ran out, unlike in many others. The air conditioner also performed flawlessly.

The food at Felicito’s was excellent, with options for breakfast, lunch, and dinner. You can dine in the restaurant or opt for room service, which was prompt and efficient. The staff were incredibly friendly and accommodating, making us feel truly welcome. The restaurant closes at 10 p.m., but it's best to order before 9 p.m., as the kitchen sometimes shuts down earlier. Some of my favorites included the calamares, Sinugba na Tangigue (grilled fish), and halo-halo for dessert. The drinks were well-crafted too—my top picks were the dragon fruit shake and the Bern Paradise cocktail, a vibrant bluish-greenish concoction that tastes delightfully fruity.

Overall, our stay was amazing, and we’ll definitely be returning. Thank you to the Waterworld Cebu staff for making it so memorable! || author=Cary 1013; rating=2; time=2025-10-11T01:20:22.642313754Z; relative=6 months ago; text=This hotel was a very bad experience for us. We reserved a  family suite room, but it is not spotless; the toilet smells a little, and the drinking water is only refilled at the water station. It would have been lovely if they had offered safe and clean bottled water. Three bags of tea were given, and further requests were not permitted. There is only a bin in the bathroom, not in the living room, kitchen or bedrooms. The hand wash is watery and contains very little soap, while the body wash and shampoo are in a pump bottle that is difficult to pump and is mounted on the wall. No laundry bag and the wifi is so slow.

The food at their restaurant is fairly restricted and unappealing, so there is nothing to look forward to.

The hotel guests' waterpark access gate's shower room is closed, and the water park's large slides, wave pool, and lazy pool are all not functioning. The waterpark is rusting all around. I got wounded on one of the slides.

Because my kids enjoy swimming and staycations, we frequently stay at hotels in Manila and Cebu. We are  dissatisfied with our experience in Waterworld Hotel comparison to our previous hotel reservations, which is why I am writing this review. Most hotels offer excellent service at substantially lower rates.

name: La Parisienne Cebu
types: european_restaurant, bakery, food_store, restaurant, store, point_of_interest, food, establishment
address: 371 Gorordo Ave, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Mery Ghyrzle Ann M. Bargaso; rating=4; time=2026-02-17T14:40:36.727178491Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Food:
La Vie Pizza is delicious in flavor; however, it was served cold when it arrived at our table. We asked to have it reheated, and it was promptly attended to.

The Mixed Seafood Pasta was unfortunately disappointing. Although it was freshly cooked and served hot, the dish was overwhelmingly sour and had a strong fishy taste that overpowered everything else. I added hot sauce to help balance the strong sour and fishy flavors so I can eat a little more.

I also noticed that the pastries were displayed openly, which may raise some concerns about food hygiene.

Service:
I called the restaurant prior to arriving. The staff answered professionally, though the tone sounded slightly annoyed.

The parking attendant was very helpful and courteous.

Ordering took quite a while because the card payment machine was not working, and the previous customer’s payment took time to process.

On a positive note, the waiter was attentive and acted immediately when we requested to have the pizza reheated.

Atmosphere:
The restaurant has a lovely French-inspired ambiance, which creates a pleasant and cozy dining experience. || author=Sir Chad; rating=5; time=2026-01-08T02:05:34.470579144Z; relative=3 months ago; text=La Parisienne in Cebu is a lovely spot with a cozy and elegant Parisian-inspired vibe. The place feels warm and inviting perfect for a relaxed coffee break, brunch, or a quiet meal with friends. The interiors are charming without being over the top.

Their pastries and food are well-prepared and satisfying, paired nicely with good coffee. Service is friendly and attentive, making the overall experience pleasant from start to finish.

Overall, La Parisienne is a great café in Cebu if you’re looking for good food, a calm atmosphere, and a touch of European charm. Definitely worth visiting. || author=Miriam Cinconiegue; rating=5; time=2026-02-19T07:52:02.980840762Z; relative=2 months ago; text=The ambience, the choice of music, the staff, the food, the service and the value for money call for a five-star rating. They have wide selection of wine but beer is limited. I loved their gelato as well. || author=The Real Review; rating=5; time=2025-11-11T12:40:11.720620168Z; relative=5 months ago; text=The experience at this establishment was truly remarkable and memorable. Visitors are naturally drawn to their impressive wine cellar, which is beautifully designed and exudes an inviting atmosphere. The cellar is a focal point, capturing the attention of many with its extensive collection and elegant presentation. However, while the wine cellar garners a lot of attention, it seems that their culinary offerings do not receive as much spotlight. Despite this, the food menu is quite commendable, offering a diverse and well-curated selection that complements the wine selection perfectly. The dishes are crafted with care, showcasing quality ingredients and thoughtful preparation, making the dining experience equally delightful as the wine tasting. Overall, the establishment provides a well-rounded experience that should not be overlooked, with both the wine and food deserving of recognition and appreciation. || author=Ioannis Manos; rating=5; time=2026-02-15T13:29:30.805307891Z; relative=2 months ago; text=It was very nice  place . name: Mt. Kang-Irag Campsite, Sirao
address: 9VXC+RJ8, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=SAPIO, AIVAN CHRISTOPHER; rating=5; time=2025-11-11T22:18:29.307685494Z; relative=5 months ago; text=Mt. Kang-Irag Campsite, located in the highlands of Cebu, offers a serene and refreshing escape for nature enthusiasts and adventure seekers. The site is known for its panoramic views, cool mountain breeze, and peaceful atmosphere, making it ideal for overnight camping and early morning hikes. Trails leading to the campsite are moderately challenging, requiring proper footwear and preparation, especially during wet conditions.

Facilities are basic, emphasizing a close-to-nature experience. Visitors are encouraged to bring their own supplies and practice responsible tourism. Overall, Mt. Kang-Irag provides a rewarding outdoor experience for those seeking tranquility and scenic beauty in a natural setting. || author=Die or Never; rating=5; time=2026-02-19T00:38:18.640258366Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We went there during Typhoon Basyang. The place was very quiet because there were no other groups—just us. It felt like it was exclusively ours. There was a bit of fear thinking about the storm, but we chose to enjoy it since we were already there. The adventure was truly memorable. Camping despite the storm. || author=yosi pateno; rating=5; time=2025-09-27T11:47:35.638804728Z; relative=7 months ago; text=We rode our bikes from Mandaue through Budlaan, Buak, and Lower Malubog, taking the narrow road near Holiday Pools. The journey was tough—narrow alleys forced us to carry, push, and walk our bikes at times. Locals kindly helped us navigate the final stretch. Reaching the destination, the breathtaking views instantly washed away our exhaustion. After enjoying a meal and relaxing for an hour, we headed back home via man path way with ragged terrain to  Cambios langub Road. || author=Dan Paqueo; rating=5; time=2025-11-17T03:06:47.016807342Z; relative=5 months ago; text=The road I was taking from parking area right below the Campsite was a bit harder to walk through since typhoon Tino eroded the soil and big rocks remained along the trail. Lisud pasaka kay bug-at ako Euc Electric Uni Cycle  going to the campsite.

Nevertheless the view from the top was breathtaking and worth the effort of reaching it. 💕 || author=Lysa Wookie; rating=5; time=2025-05-19T07:45:29.521433Z; relative=11 months ago; text=It took us 1 hour and 32 minutes to get there. The trail from Budlaan River to Mt. Kan-irag is a beginner-friendly hike, and the scenery is breathtaking. There’s a fee of ₱50, and they have plenty of cottages around where you can relax without any additional charge.

I tried Pork Barrel pork steak, Weng’s quail egg siomai, Nata’s egg tart, Guadalupe’s lechon, Maki’s, fish tofu/lobster balls, and sugarcane juice. So far, I really enjoyed the sugarcane juice and the lobster/fish tofu on a stick. The rest were okay to try once.
The place was too packed and cramped for my liking, but it’s ideal for groups of friends. I hope they add more standing tables that are good for one or two people.
Would I revisit? Probably not. I’d likely try other night markets next time. || author=Jon Ida; rating=5; time=2025-11-17T05:46:33.116857627Z; relative=5 months ago; text=Visited it for the first time on a rainy night, and while it was definitely a bit of a hassle - with the unpaved, uneven paths, water puddles, and some areas without a roof - it somehow added a cozy charm to the place. The ground can get muddy, but the food stalls stay lively, and the variety is solid - barbecue, ramen, sisig, desserts, basically anything you’re craving. The rain even makes the hot food taste better, especially the ramen and anything grilled. Seating can get a bit tight since everyone crowds under the covered spots, but overall it’s still a chill place to eat, hang out, and enjoy comfort food while listening to the rain in the background. || author=Christian Ray Aganap; rating=4; time=2026-02-19T21:48:29.627522247Z; relative=2 months ago; text=The place was okay, but it was jam-packed. sample_reviews:
author=Audrey Ruffles; rating=5; time=2026-01-28T07:41:16.295088591Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Cebu Safari is perfect for both kids and adults. My child had an amazing time seeing the animals up close and riding the safari bus. It was fun, exciting, and also educational. The animals look well cared for and the spacious enclosures make the experience even more enjoyable.

I brought my children there, and it felt like the perfect place to spend quality time together, surrounded by nature, with animals that are clearly well cared for and respected. The area is well preserved, the food was great, and getting around was easy and convenient. Even with many visitors that day, the staff handled everything smoothly, making the whole trip comfortable, safe, and enjoyable for families like ours. || author=David Rhodes (Big Dave); rating=5; time=2026-01-26T05:46:17.946639454Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Very beautiful and relaxing adventure. All animals looked happy and healthy a sign they were all well taken care of. Loved every exhibit and display. Stayed in the very comfortable and quiet one bedroom cabin. || author=Paul Hongkung; rating=4; time=2025-12-05T11:07:36.354179351Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Amazing place for birds and bird show. The birds sanctuary were totally out of the ordinary. An incredible experience. Apart from that, it is a normal zoo. Seeing many giraffes is a wonderful sight.

If you are visiting, start with the white lion show. Then you can save time on watching others. Otherwise it's a whole waiting and walking game. || author=Lean Gomez; rating=5; time=2025-10-13T03:12:02.171716896Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Located about two hours away from Cebu City, Cebu Safari and Adventure Park remains one of the best attractions in Northern Cebu. It’s a perfect spot for families, especially those with kids who love animals. You can get there by commuting, but since we had a child with us, we decided to rent a car with a driver for convenience and it definitely made the trip more comfortable.

This was actually my second time visiting, and I can confidently say that I enjoyed it much more this time around. The weather was clear and sunny, and we arrived earlier, which allowed us to explore the park fully without rushing. We reached the entrance at exactly 8:00 AM, even before the park opened, and to our surprise, there were already a number of guests waiting.

We booked our tickets through Klook, which made the entry process hassle-free and even included free Macaw Bird Feeding. We still lined up at the counter, though, hoping to get a child discount for my cousin, unfortunately, they strictly follow height-based rates (36 inches and below for discounted child rate), and my cousin was already at 40 inches, so we had to pay the regular price. It was fine and understandable since the staff were polite and consistent with their policies.

The Welcome Pavilion (reception area) was spacious, well-maintained, and clean. There’s also Wi-Fi available in the reception, though we didn’t get to test if it reached other parts of the park. Comfort rooms are conveniently located throughout the entire safari, and I appreciate how they’re always clean and accessible.

Ticket prices may seem quite high at first, but once you experience the park, you’ll realize it’s worth every peso. The quality of maintenance, care for animals, and overall experience justify the price. The trams are readily available, making it easy to move between different zones of the park without too much walking under the sun.

I truly appreciate how the safari is laid out. Even with the large number of guests, it never feels overcrowded. Every area is well-marked, and you can always find helpful staff members who are approachable and knowledgeable.

For food, we had lunch at The Outpost, where we ordered their set meals that come with rice and drinks. The food wasn’t particularly filling, and the prices were a bit high for the portion size and quality, but it was enough to refuel us after hours of walking. Generally, food and drinks inside the park are priced higher, which is expected for attractions of this scale.

There’s ample parking space near the entrance and tram stations, which is very convenient. The overall cleanliness of the park is commendable, from pathways to rest stops, everything is tidy and well-kept.

What I love most is seeing that the animals are well cared for. Their enclosures are spacious and designed to resemble natural habitats, allowing them to move freely. It’s reassuring to see how the park balances tourism with animal welfare.

Overall, Cebu Safari and Adventure Park continues to be one of Cebu’s most impressive destinations. It’s a full-day adventure that’s fun, educational, and family-friendly. Whether it’s your first visit or a return trip like mine, it’s an experience that never loses its magic. 🌳🦁🦓

Could take an hour or more just to get to this waterfalls but it's still worth it.

There are no entrance fees. The comfort rooms are free but you can donate any amount for the usage.

Soaps and shampoos are not allowed when in the water as it could kill marine life in the river. || author=jacky Bajenting; rating=4; time=2021-05-25T13:56:16.603475Z; relative=4 years ago; text=I will give 4 Because of the potential however that road going to the place is so bumpy as of the writing , there is a road concreting project that is going on. It is advisable to go there early and with motorcycle since the area is uphill..in fact the driver turned off the aircon. It is advisable not to go there during rainy days.but if you wish for an adventure, you may walk from the brgy captains house going to the falls. You may experience a 80 0r 75 degrees angle downhill that will measure your endurance. Of course that might give you a muscle pain afterward. If the concreting of the road will be finished surely there will be more tourist might go back there as well.thanks for the people of brgy tabili for helping us in our trip.

sample_reviews:
author=The Mighty Trogdor; rating=2; time=2024-02-18T00:37:43.332594Z; relative=2 years ago; text=Not really worth it.  See the pics.  Small 10 ft waterfall with a maybe 6ft deep pool at base. Difficult to get to.  Lots of trash left around the waterfall really detracting from nature.

To get there I  drove along the road, past the location on the map until I nearly reached the valley floor.  At a steep driveway on the valley side I walked down the driveway, crossed a stream, took a left along a pathway that borders some farmland.  Past the first field you'll veer left toward the stream.  Now it's a mix of trails on the side of the stream or actually walking in the stream.  Stay by the water and you'll make it there.

The waterfall area itself is quite precarious and difficult to get to base.

There were about ten locals enjoying the place when I arrived. || author=Artur Szczygielski; rating=5; time=2026-02-22T06:02:32.938310403Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Great place, we had great Time with local people.. || author=Maria Christine Colmenares; rating=3; time=2024-05-22T14:50:55.898732Z; relative=a year ago; text=The place is beautiful, but visitors keep on leaving their trash behind thus marring the beauty if this place. name: Balay’g sawa Falls
address: G887+7R6, Samboan, Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Xandra Mae Villacarlos-Duran; rating=5; time=2025-05-13T02:58:36.804884Z; relative=11 months ago; text=This has to be my 2nd favorite waterfall in South Cebu—but be warned: this is not your typical tourist destination. What you’ll find here is raw beauty wrapped in mystery... and a whole lot of folklore.

⚠️ Warning: Local lore + eerie experiences ahead! ⚠️

This waterfall was opened to the public years ago but is now officially closed, mostly due to stories of it being “taw-an”—a place said to be inhabited or guarded by spirits. Locals consider it sacred, and visitors are strongly urged to show respect. This is not a place for loud barkadas, parties, or reckless fun.

The trail felt like stepping into another dimension. As we hiked down, we heard faint music—no source, no explanation—just eerie sounds in the thick forest. The moss-covered steps and dense trees made us say, “This is how Americans in movies die.” The atmosphere? Otherworldly.

The trail itself is dilapidated, steep, and slippery. Vehicles must be left at the small payag at the top, and the rest is a foot journey. Definitely not suitable for seniors, infants, or small kids.

When we got home to Lapu-Lapu, our landlady, residing from Malabuyoc,  was shook to the bones upon learning we went. She told us of children who had gone missing there before. According to locals, the falls’ guardians were angered by visitors who disrespected the site—leading to the pools drying up, the flow weakening, and the place falling into neglect.

That said, we were happy to see signs of natural recovery during our visit. The area still holds its magic. But sadly, some people leave trash behind or vandalize rocks. (To whoever wrote on the stones—what were you thinking?)

And then the creepiest part: while descending, we heard two people laughing nearby. But when we reached the bottom—no one. No voices. No people. Just us and the falls. 100% goosebumps.

🙏 Please—if you do go: do not destroy the place.
❌ No loud music
❌ No trash
❌ No graffiti
✅ Just respect.

This place demands reverence.

Ratings:
🧗‍♀️ Trail Difficulty: 8/10
🌄 Scenic Beauty: 10/10
🧼 Cleanliness: 9.1/10 (nature’s work, not ours)
🪨 Mossy Stones: 6/10
💧 Slipperiness: 8/10
🌊 Waterfall Strength: 7/10 – calm and peaceful
🐍 Snakes Spotted: 5/10 – skins and nests only, no actual snakes

We honestly believe the energy we brought—respectful, quiet, grateful—blessed our experience. It only started raining (very hard!!!) after we got back to the payag where we left our cars. It felt like the guardians let us enjoy the place safely... and held off the downpour until we were back on top.

Thank you, Lord, for the gift of nature.
May everyone who visits remember that this place is more than just scenery—it's alive, it's sacred, and it deserves our care.

But if you will only disrespect it, please don't come. || author=Alfonso R; rating=5; time=2023-03-31T11:18:47.016253Z; relative=3 years ago; text=It's really a hidden gem.
First, start where the other comment says. It that small porch.
Then to avoid crossing the house, you must go down the path where I point the finger. Once you arrive to another path, turn left and go up. You will notice the house is behind you.
Keep walking, you will see the shed.
After the shed you will see the steps going down. You will also hear the falls. Some of the path is gone but you can continue around it. Go to the very bottom where there is no wire. Just continue a little more to the left that there it is.
Quite beautiful and a really good swim. There are different pools in different levels. || author=ted thor; rating=5; time=2023-08-01T00:46:09.418561Z; relative=2 years ago; text=A small, almost 'forgotten' waterfall. No tourists. No entrance fees. No guide fees. Just a short 10-15 walk to get to the waterfall. Just park at the abandoned white hut and walk

sample_reviews:
author=Jon Ida; rating=5; time=2024-08-27T16:23:05.883274Z; relative=a year ago; text=Dayhag Falls is a serene natural attraction, surrounded by lush greenery and offering a peaceful escape into nature. The cascading water creates a soothing atmosphere, perfect for relaxation or light hiking. Though the trail is accessible to most, it can be slippery, so visitors should proceed with caution. || author=Green AI; rating=3; time=2023-05-07T00:24:12.970951Z; relative=2 years ago; text=Dayhag Falls is a popular tourist destination located in Boljoon, south of Cebu. While it is reasonably far from Cebu, it is easily accessible, and the Google Maps pin accurately guides visitors to the site. However, visitors should expect some delays on their journey due to ongoing road construction in the municipality of Argao and a few kilometers from Upper Becerril, where Dayhag Falls is located.

It is worth noting that Dayhag Falls was heavily damaged during Typhoon Odette, and many of its features were destroyed. Currently, the last pool remains unusable, but both the first and second pools are operational. Despite this, the aesthetic features of the site, such as the pathways, flags, cleanliness, dirt stairs, ferns, and the large "avatar size" tree, remain the same.

To maintain the facilities and keep the Dayhag Falls clean, there is a 20-peso entrance fee. The funds generated from the entrance fee are used for the upkeep of the dressing room, bath, and comfort room.

One thing to be aware of is the presence of huge billboards warning visitors of flash floods, not only in the vicinity of Dayhag Falls but throughout the municipality as well. Therefore, visitors should exercise caution when visiting, especially during the rainy season.

Overall, the whole experience of visiting Dayhag Falls is fun, despite the damage caused by the typhoon. The natural beauty of the site, combined with the efforts to maintain its cleanliness and facilities, makes it a worthwhile visit for tourists looking for a unique and enjoyable outdoor experience. || author=Jui-Yu Chuang; rating=5; time=2023-08-12T14:26:33.710473Z; relative=2 years ago; text=No tourist here!

sample_reviews:
author=Raven Plaza; rating=1; time=2026-03-08T11:20:50.710061809Z; relative=a month ago; text=Visited this beach resort recently and unfortunately the experience was disappointing.

The entrance fee is ₱150 per head, which feels expensive considering I’ve been to a much larger white sand beach that only charged ₱30 per person. The pricing just doesn’t match the quality of the facilities.

They also charge corkage for camping chairs, which is overpriced at ₱75, especially when the chairs they rent out are just basic monobloc plastic chairs.

The changing areas and bathrooms were very poor. There is no privacy in the changing area, and this issue was already mentioned in another review a year ago, yet it seems management still hasn’t addressed it. The CR and shower had no running water, the area was dirty, poorly maintained, and had no lights, whether male or female.

Another concern is the number of people smoking around the beach, with cigarette butts scattered on the sand, which ruins the environment and the experience.

The only positive thing I noticed was the presence of lifeguards, although the megaphone they use is unclear and poor quality.

Overall, the resort feels overpriced for what it offers. With the high entrance fee, corkage charges, and poorly maintained bathrooms, the management really needs to improve the facilities and address the privacy and cleanliness issues. || author=Josh Arden Lim; rating=5; time=2024-10-17T09:20:02.964475Z; relative=a year ago; text=For ₱100, you get to go to this beach! Ride was ₱15 from the “Crossing” area in Marigondon. You will be riding with others in this tricycle.

This is a good beach to go when you just want to relax and not be with a big crowd. Water was shallow with the low tide. Water was a bit warm. The sand is mixed with seaweed and crushed seashells. Beach front is a bit rocky but gets softer as you go closer to the ocean. A good place with very underrated beauty!

You can bring with you food and drinks, just be sure to clean up! Pets are welcome! Nearby, there is a mini port where you can go for island-hopping in 3 islands in groups with prices ranging from ₱2500.00 to ₱3,500.00. Prices and availability depends on the day. || author=Al Jay Mejos; rating=5; time=2024-08-01T04:14:35.086298Z; relative=a year ago; text=It was great. The sand was white. There were cottages. There was a life guard. There were plenty of guards. They had a pool but unfortunately they would only allow renters of their exclusive rooms to use the pools. The entrance was a hundred pesos and there is a separate fee for the cottages.

They also have showers and restrooms but to use the showers there was some sort of payment required and the water flow was so weak. The toilets are for use of every one but it is not as clean as can be desired, it is like using a public restroom altogether.

Anyways, we had fun and we enjoyed it so much. The water is clear during the earlier half of the day but after some time as people frolic in the water, the suspended sands make it murky. The best part is there are floodlights illuminating the water so you feel safe even if it's night. The cottages aren't very pricey ranging from 1300 to 1900. Entrance is just 150. The only thing I didn't quite like is that their water in the bathroom that you use to wash off is seawater not freshwater so you'll still feel icky even after taking a shower. Not very demure. || author=Cuevas, Sarah Jean M.; rating=1; time=2025-12-22T03:31:09.325005008Z; relative=4 months ago; text=⭐☆☆☆☆ Very Disappointing Experience – Serious Privacy and Safety Issue

I visited Vaño Beach Resort on December 21, 2025 to attend a Christmas party with my partner’s team. While I understood and paid all required fees (entrance, parking, and shower fees), the overall experience was extremely disappointing due to poor management and lack of guest safety.

While using the shower facility, I was completely naked when an unknown male abruptly opened the shower curtain, exposing me without my consent. Because of the open setup of the shower area, other guests lining up outside were also in a position where they could have seen me, which was deeply embarrassing and distressing.

What made this worse was that the staff member collecting the shower fee was stationed directly in front of the shower area yet failed to prevent the incident or take immediate action. The shower facilities lacked basic privacy and safety measures—no proper doors, locks, or clear signage indicating occupancy—despite collecting a separate fee for its use.

This incident reflects serious negligence in terms of management, staff supervision, and facility maintenance. Guests deserve basic privacy, safety, and respect, especially in paid facilities.

Unfortunately, due to this experience, I cannot recommend this resort. I hope management takes this feedback seriously and makes the necessary improvements to ensure such incidents do not happen again.

sample_reviews:
author=roger.b chang; rating=5; time=2026-02-12T04:58:08.543137776Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Nice public beach clear water, a few small restaurants and soft and nice sand, if you go to Camotes you should visit this beautiful beach || author=Colin CW; rating=1; time=2026-02-18T07:54:04.696007207Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Checked in Feb 18 2026 for 10 day stay.

If you stay nearby, i suggest you eat here as its cheaper. || author=Brett O; rating=5; time=2025-04-16T07:04:31.931060Z; relative=a year ago; text=What a beach!! name: Baigad Lagoon Beach
address: Baigad, Bantayan, Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Shemuel Aldea; rating=3; time=2025-10-11T13:32:09.667605040Z; relative=6 months ago; text=We visited this place a few days after the storm, maybe that’s why our experience wasn’t that great. The beachfront was filled with seaweeds and trash, and most areas weren’t really ideal for swimming. I’d say it’s better suited for those who just want to relax and enjoy the view, since there’s not much else to do around.

If you’re being practical, it might be best to skip this one for now. The entrance fee felt a bit pricey, and there’s also an extra charge if you want to ride the pump boat.

Overall, we didn’t quite enjoy our time at Baigad, but I can see its potential, with proper maintenance, it could really be a beautiful spot. || author=Maya; rating=4; time=2025-11-04T15:44:07.501651108Z; relative=5 months ago; text=Nice place. Not crowded on weekdays.
You can walk or ride a boat if you have companion. As a single, for da walk lang habang nagmumuni muni sa mahabang white sand beach. 😄 (see last photo)

250 entrance fee btw. || author=Marco Salvador; rating=1; time=2025-12-29T01:45:59.969950477Z; relative=3 months ago; text=the place can be really looks enchanted and amazing , however , even though you have you have to pay an entrance fee the area is full of trash i wanted to swim but I don't feel really comfortable to do it.. pity..it could be a really nice place to stay || author=Kleinberg Fletcher Tabel; rating=3; time=2025-10-17T08:51:33.653253460Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Though Baigad Lagoon isn’t a separate island, it’s often included in Bantayan’s island-hopping tours since it’s best accessed by sea. The place has its charm — a peaceful lagoon where crystal-clear waters blend with the calm sea and mangroves, creating a serene, postcard-like scene.

However, that serenity comes with a ₱250 entrance fee, and honestly, it feels a little steep for what’s offered. After experiencing the stunning Virgin Island, Baigad feels more like a filler stop than a highlight. There’s a swing that’s more ornamental than functional, a few tables by the lagoon, and a small restaurant — but the menu prices are eyebrow-raising, especially for such a simple setup.

Still, if you’re the type who likes to complete the full Bantayan experience, it’s worth a quick stop — just to say you’ve been. The lagoon is undeniably pretty and photogenic, but if you’re expecting another Virgin Island-level paradise, you might leave saying, “That’s it?” || author=greg mesina; rating=5; time=2025-09-24T08:40:17.484690048Z; relative=7 months ago; text=The Baigad Lagoon Beach has amazing view and it can offer many instagramble places. You can choose to swim at the beach or at the lagoon side. The water there is not so much salty. You can choose to walk or ride a boat for 150 per person back and forth. Then the entrace fee for Baigad Lagoon is P250 per head
For me it is worth the price.

name: Orongan Beach Resort
address: 2WC4+JJR, San Remigio, Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Finity Vhip; rating=3; time=2025-06-16T03:11:42.249948Z; relative=10 months ago; text=Room – 7/10
We stayed in Room 7 for ₱3,000, and honestly, it was a pretty good deal. The room had 1 queen-sized bed and a double deck, with a pillow and blanket for each bed. They also added a stand fan since the aircon wasn’t that cold, but it was still okay overall.

Bathroom (CR) – 5/10
There’s a shower, but sadly it wasn’t working properly. The water pressure from the faucet was super weak, which made it a bit of a hassle.

Service – 10/10
The staff were super friendly and respectful! I really appreciated how quick they were at collecting the trash—felt like they were doing it every hour. Big thumbs up for that!

Ambience – 8/10
The sea had a lot of trash, which was a little disappointing, but the view was still beautiful. The place also has a bunch of stray cats and dogs roaming around, but they’re harmless. Just be careful in feeding them - they might scratch you.
Oh, and there are tons of hammocks—probably around 10!—and two cute wooden swings, perfect for relaxing.

Would I come back?
Definitely yes 🧡

P.S. Just a heads-up, the road going to the beach is super bumpy, so prepare for a rough ride! || author=Felicio, Ailah; rating=2; time=2025-08-26T22:04:24.035247390Z; relative=8 months ago; text=Nice view. Calm waters. White sand. BUT where we stayed at is next to a cemetery. Eerie at night. Please bring your own food and cooking supplies. || author=JJ CBMD; rating=5; time=2024-03-05T09:23:57.524569Z; relative=2 years ago; text=So beautiful beach, whitesand and clear water, what can I say. Staff are super friendly and very accommodating specially Ate Cherry and other Lady that always serve our food and attend our needs. It was not plan to get here but the other beach resort is fully booked and only accept online which didn't say at all in their site. But anyway we are so happy that Kuya the tricycle driver suggest to come in here instead and we are so grateful.

They have regular room P2,000 ($40) for two but can be also family of 5 or 6  but price is vary.
Room with balcony facing the beach is P2,500($50) for two.

They serve food starts at P110 ($2)
combo meal ex: egg, rice and corned beef.
They also have mini mart but you can bring food no corkage and you can cook or grill food in their open kitchen area.

They also offer party table, cottages, tent or table and chair only, so many choices it could be your alone or 20 people, they can accommodate as long as you call them ahead bcoz they're always fully booked.
We are so lucky to have vacant room in that day and checkout next day at 10am, and heard that there are people already booked for our room at 2pm.

The beach is absolutely amazing and excellent staff. Worth the travel and stay and money. Check this out! || author=Shaeni Loveine; rating=2; time=2025-06-21T03:45:11.563918981Z; relative=10 months ago; text=The beach wasn’t very impressive. There were a TON of garbage on the shore of the beach. As in plastic wrappers of junk food & what-not. There were also a looooot of stray dogs. Nothing against stray dogs, but most of them had mange, and there’s always the risk of accidentally stepping on 💩 & stuff.

Travelled very far & didn’t end up swimming bc of the water’s condition.

Not entirely the resort’s fault since it was monsoon season during this. But there were no pools either so we literally didn’t have any other choice. Paid 6k for a 10pax room & it only had 1 bathroom 🥲.

Wi-fi was okay. the quality of the bunk beds were bad too, had a veryyyy difficult time getting up and down, my feet would hurt after. || author=Anne Pilapil; rating=5; time=2023-10-16T06:43:13.934405Z; relative=2 years ago; text=Enjoyed my recent stay in this place! I do recommend the place!😁

We were supposed to go to Bantayan Island but preferred to stay here instead when we saw the white beach and the clear waters. Aside from that, the rate is very affordable for a family as big as ours. Plus, if you brought a tent, they allow the use of tent too!

I sqy if you want peace and quiet always schedule trip before a long holiday or after it. Less people. 😉

They are now accepting walkins but still good to text beforehand specially if you intend to rent rooms and cottages.

Price range:
Family room (6 person) - 3k
Tent area rent - 150
Tables and chairs rental (4 chairs only) - 200
Big Tent with tables and chairs - 800
150 per person if excess on the allowed person in the room.
Entrance fee - 150 for overnight use, 75 for day use.

sample_reviews:
author=Emily L.; rating=4; time=2026-02-25T14:09:12.837348349Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Internet is the only reason for 4 out of 5 stars, their wifi is so slow and sometimes doesn’t work 2G or 5G -it’s spotty at peak times.

That being said, everything else is wonderful. The staff are exceptional!! The breakfast buffet is stellar! The pizza and other menu items are good as well. Lots of options for all types of people. The dinner buffet is a bit pricey but should be tried at least once during your stay- we discovered that each day is different. For example Wednesday- on the beach with fresh fish cooked in front of you, and always with a live band which was fun.

The pools are wonderful too! Our kids had a great time at each of the three pools but spent the most time with the slide. Each pool is large and never felt crowded and were always clean.

name: Caohagan Beach
address: Caohagan Beach, Lapu-Lapu, Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Kleinberg Fletcher Tabel; rating=4; time=2025-10-04T11:12:53.286127275Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Caohagan was the second island me and my girlfriend visited on our Mactan Island Hopping adventure — and it felt like the kind of stop where time simply slows down. I couldn’t help but thank God for blessing us with perfect weather that day. Just 24 hours earlier, Cebu was drenched in heavy rain, but now the skies were spotless — the sea a glassy shade of turquoise that seemed to glow.

As we approached, our boatman carefully docked on the shallows — a thoughtful move, considering how shallow Caohagan’s waters are. Some tourists were being fetched by locals in smaller boats (for a small fee), while others waded all the way from their boats to the beach, walking barefoot across the clear water, bravely trusting the sand beneath them not to hide a sea urchin.

The moment we stepped ashore, the island’s charm unfolded in full color. Local kids played and waved at passing visitors, laughter echoing in the air. Our boatman guided us through the island’s small barangay — a heartwarming glimpse into everyday island life. We passed by sari-sari stores, wooden homes, a humble chapel, and a basketball court before finally reaching the white-sand beach that everyone talks about.

The beach itself is beautifully raw — powdery white sand, rustic cottages you can freely rest in, and locals selling freshly caught seafood right by the shore. A wind vane marked “Caohagan Peace” turns gently above it all, perfectly symbolizing the island’s serene, unhurried rhythm. The water here is incredibly shallow — almost like a mirror reflecting the clouds — which makes it ideal for beach bumming, not swimming. To swim, you’d have to walk far into deeper water, and honestly, it’s not worth the risk.

Still, Caohagan’s beauty doesn’t need depth — its magic lies on the surface. It’s a place that invites you to pause, to smile, to breathe. If you’re going island hopping in Mactan, don’t skip this one. It’s a gentle, genuine, and soul-soothing piece of island life that reminds you what the sea is really about — peace. || author=Jamaal G; rating=5; time=2025-12-24T05:29:02.815946595Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Beautiful white sand beach, it was included in our Mactan island tour. Rocky at points so bring water shoes or flip flops. || author=Germotion - YouTube: Germotion; rating=5; time=2025-03-07T00:29:13.329270Z; relative=a year ago; text=Caohagan Beach offers pristine white fine sand and calm, flat waters, making it a perfect spot for relaxation and swimming. Local vendors add to the vibrant atmosphere, and it's an ideal location for tourists looking to enjoy a lively beach party experience. Drone flights provide stunning aerial views of the beautiful coastline, enhancing the overall visit. || author=Harish; rating=5; time=2023-09-08T07:55:19.350209Z; relative=2 years ago; text=Caohagan Island was the final stop on our three-island hopping tour, and it was a charming little gem. Its tiny size didn't diminish its appeal, featuring a beautiful sandy beach. My daughter had a blast collecting shells, and we took advantage of the reasonably priced kayaking. While we spotted jet skis, we didn't try them ourselves. What really stood out was the opportunity to shop for souvenirs made from shells, and it's crucial to bargain for the best deals. We noticed that the same souvenirs cost significantly more in shopping malls. Overall, Caohagan Island was a great place to enjoy some coconut water, indulge in souvenir shopping, and have a relaxing kayaking experience. || author=Yutaro Murota; rating=4; time=2026-01-05T04:40:11.086822835Z; relative=3 months ago; text=It was too shallow for the boat to reach the coast and had to walk long, so gave up and just enjoyed the view from far. Anyhow this place is amazing... 🍻🍻🍻🍻🍻🍻🍻 || author=Paul Ouano; rating=4; time=2020-10-30T12:44:52.613418Z; relative=5 years ago; text=The final road going into Uncle Mike's is very narrow, and worse, flood-prone. The whole road is filled with large pools of water. If you don't have a capable vehicle, do not go to this place if it's raining, or if it rained the night before, or even 2 nights before. The pools of floodwater take a long time to recede. That said, Uncle Mike's itself is a great place to hang out at, cowboy style. It is spacious, has ample parking, has several trees to take shade under, has spaces to setup a barbecue or makeshift tents. There is no sandy beachfront, instead it has a cemented edge with 3 or 4 steps going down into the water. That said, the water is clean and clear. It's a great place for paddle boarding, among other activities. Uncle Mike's is definitely good value for money, at only 20 pesos entrance fee, and 50 pesos parking. It's great for families that just want a day at the "beach", grilling some barbecue, or bringing some pusô and lechon manok.

There are also dogs roaming around the resort. || author=Kleinberg Fletcher Tabel; rating=3; time=2022-08-31T00:03:57.902250Z; relative=3 years ago; text=Barili is not known for any beautiful white sandy beaches like the ones in Moalboal, and if you come to this place, you'll see why. Though despite its lack of sand, the crystal clear water makes up for it very well.

Situated facing the Tañon strait that separates the islands of Cebu and Negros, the beach offers one of the most captivating sunsets as the reflection of the sun dips through the clear ocean below, and the beautiful island of Negros looming across the horizon.

The beach has a convenience store/restaurant/registration area that sells a variety of items from snacks, drinks, hanging rice (puso), barbecues, eggs, and even wifi voucher codes. You will learn to love these voucher codes since the area has pretty bad signal receptions if you wish to stay online.

Rooms and cottages are available. We booked one of their standard rooms good for two during our stay. It has basic amenities which is worth its price. It has a tv, shower, some free toiletries, and is air conditioned. It has a balcony that offers a charming view of the area. It is here that I enjoyed the captivating sunset.

They were kind enough to let us use their cottage for free for us to eat our dinner since we ordered some foods in their restaurant.

Sugar Beach with its white powdery sand and turquoise blue water invites you to relax and is reminiscent of an advertisement from a travel agency's advertising catalog. Because of the low tide, it's best to arrive around midday. You can go up to fifty meters into the water and enjoy the waist-high water. The many palm trees on the beach offer some shady spots. || author=Jhon Stephen Rebusa; rating=5; time=2023-07-17T02:38:44.734902Z; relative=2 years ago; text=A long stretch of pristine beach with its powder-fine sand and crystal-clear waters is ideal for a relaxing swim or simply watching the sunset. Unfortunately, the reef that once thrived in this area has been destroyed due to overfishing, particularly with the use of dynamite. As a result, there is not much marine life to observe while snorkeling. Although the practice has been banned, the damage has already been done. sample_reviews:
author=Rod Miler Gutierez; rating=5; time=2026-02-24T01:41:03.566574126Z; relative=2 months ago; text=I had such a wonderful experience at Le Jardin in Cebu City! The ambiance is elegant yet cozy, making it perfect for a relaxing meal or a special occasion. The food was beautifully presented and absolutely delicious, with flavors that truly stand out. The staff were warm, attentive, and made us feel very welcome from start to finish. Overall, it was a memorable dining experience, and I would definitely recommend Le Jardin to anyone looking for great food and a lovely atmosphere in Cebu. || author=Angelyn; rating=5; time=2025-10-20T03:06:11.565038606Z; relative=6 months ago; text=We visited Lakeview Le Jardin, and it was incredible. The garden is massive and absolutely stunning—so much lush, well-kept flora and fauna. We couldn't explore everything because it's so huge, but even a quick stroll before dinner was a treat. They even sell flowers, but we arrived a bit late and the garden staff had already left.

The restaurant's atmosphere is really nice. It's clean and well-lit, and the staff were so welcoming and attentive. We were on a motorcycle, and they were kind enough to hold our helmets for us while we were in the garden.
The food was great. The four-cheese pizza was delicious, and I loved that they served mint tea made with fresh mint from their garden. They also have craft beers, which is a nice touch.

The whole place felt so calm. The views of the mountains and cloudy skies were breathtaking, and the fog that rolled in was a beautiful bonus. I would definitely recommend it! || author=tansoflavor; rating=4; time=2025-10-17T08:26:25.633403487Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Relaxing mountain/garden views with great food options!

There's an entrance fee for the garden (P100/head), but pretty worth it considering how big the grounds are. We spent about half an hour but you can take longer to really savour the views.

The restaurant has ample seating, though it can get hot since it's an open area. Got the Fern Salad, Truffle Mushroom Pasta, and Lakeview Chicken Sandwich. Everything was superb!

Fern Salad came with a balsamic dressing, and was so refreshing. Truffle Mushroom Pasta had so much umami! Not too heavy on the "truffle" taste but the mushroom flavor is prominent. The Chicken Sandwich was huge, with a great slaw and super crispy chicken.

Wasn't expecting much for a spot kinda far from the city, but was pleasantly surprised by how well kept the gardens were and with the menu spread. Would come back for the food, and to enjoy a relaxing morning or the sunset. || author=Clark Ian Olivar; rating=5; time=2025-12-09T11:42:10.482485637Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Lovely place. 9/10 date for nature lovers. name: TOPS - SKAI360°

sample_reviews:
author=Carlo Abadingo; rating=5; time=2025-09-28T01:15:32.465687977Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Compared to my previous visit before the whole face lift, TOPS is more accessible now and convenient. A lot of stores and restos already and bus availability for transport. || author=K Garcia; rating=5; time=2025-02-22T12:28:30.479805Z; relative=a year ago; text=**Skai 360: Cebu City’s Elevated Masterpiece of Views and Experiences**

Additional entrance fee of 50 pesos

Perched 2,000 feet above sea level in the lush highlands of Busay, Skai 360 has redefined Cebu’s tourism landscape by offering a panoramic 360-degree vantage point that eclipses the famed Tops Lookout in both elevation and immersive appeal. From its modern, glass-railed observation deck, visitors gaze upon a breathtaking tapestry of contrasts: the glittering skyline of Cebu City, with landmarks like IT Park and historic Colon Street, merges seamlessly with the untamed beauty of the Central Cebu Mountain Range to the west and the azure expanse of the Tañon Strait to the east. The venue’s innovative design dissolves visual barriers, creating an almost aerial sensation, while interactive telescopes and audio guides enrich the experience with stories of Cebu’s history, from ancient Rajahnates to Spanish colonial influences. At sunset, the *Golden Hour Deck* captivates as the sun dips behind mist-crowned peaks, painting the sky in fiery amber and rose hues, while after dark, the *Starlit Terrace* transforms the city into a mesmerizing galaxy of twinkling lights. Thrill-seekers gravitate to the suspended glass walkway, where transparent flooring amplifies the adrenaline of hovering above the landscape, while photographers flock to capture time-lapses of dawn’s fog rolling over the metropolis or golden hour reflections on the city’s glass towers. Culinary delights await at the *Cloud Nine Café*, where local flavors like *sinuglaw* (grilled pork and ceviche) and rich *tablea* hot chocolate are savored against the backdrop of endless horizons, while romantic *Sky Pods* offer private, star-lit dinners with curated menus and live acoustic melodies. Families thrive at the *Explorer’s Corner*, where augmented reality displays and scavenger hunts engage children with Cebu’s ecosystems and landmarks, blending education with adventure. Seasonal events, such as sunrise yoga sessions or vibrant cultural performances, add dynamic layers to the experience, ensuring no two visits are alike. Skai 360’s elevation not only guarantees cooler temperatures, providing a refreshing escape from the city’s heat, but also positions it as a gateway to distant wonders, like the silhouette of Bohol’s Chocolate Hills or the Camotes Sea’s shimmering islands on clear days. Its accessibility—just a short scenic drive from Cebu City—makes it a favorite for both tourists and locals, while its sustainable architecture, crafted with eco-conscious materials, minimizes environmental impact. Unlike Tops, Skai 360 merges luxury, adventure, and cultural storytelling into a single destination, offering ever-shifting perspectives of Cebu’s beauty, whether under the bold midday sun, the soft glow of twilight, or the magic of a starlit night. By day, the venue highlights the city’s vibrant energy and surrounding natural drama; by night, it becomes a serene oasis above the urban pulse, redefining how travelers connect with the Philippines’ “Queen City of the South.” As a must-visit attraction, Skai 360 stands not just as a viewpoint, but as a celebration of Cebu’s past, present, and boundless horizons. || author=Mae Anne Villaro; rating=5; time=2025-05-04T12:54:52.535778Z; relative=11 months ago; text=So nice and incredible moment there ♥️I love the view very much 😍 Such a very fresh air . sample_reviews:
author=Gigiwa; rating=5; time=2026-01-14T20:57:26.803274800Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Kudos to the amazing restaurant crew! Everyone was incredibly accommodating and kind. I made our reservation through Facebook, and the process was smooth—they responded right away. When we arrived, I asked if they could sing “Happy Birthday” to my mom, and they gladly did. I originally viewed the restaurant and found the prices acceptable for the location. But upon arrival you have to pay an entrance fee for entering, and then another fee for ascending to the top floor.

Further the portion size was very small. I payed  540 pesos for Beef Salpicao. Rice was not included in the tiny bowl, the meat was tender, but it was also bland. I think some more onion and seasoning could have helped with portion size and flavor.
I payed extra for rice.

My friends got the mushroom truffle noodles and we're also extremely disappointed by the portion size. They told us it's the new plating, but if the new plating is a big plate and only 1/3 covered by a tiny amount of noodles, I think it's suboptimal.

Nedless to say we were all disappointed and felt a little mislead and scammed. The old Fotos looked a lot better, and showed bigger portions.
We expressed our dissatisfaction with service, but they were unwilling to accommodate our issues.

The 360 is a separate entrance fee though. You have to pay for entrance and parking. Then once you're inside, you have to pay for the 360 view deck. There are some store closed, probably they open it during some occasions and festival like Sinulog. Boss Cofee crew are awesome, fast and patience. There are entitled customers and I saw first hand how Boss Cofee crew handle it professionally with patienc. Boss Cofee have access to 2nd floor. Keep it up. They have live music, probably local band except for the one guy (foreigner). He's good though and natural. I was amazed how he tried his best to song tagalog music and embrace the Bisdak culture. || author=Jessie Cris Tindoc; rating=5; time=2026-01-24T07:18:06.614133928Z; relative=3 months ago; text=If you are looking for an unforgettable dining experience in Cebu, Top of Cebu is a must-visit destination. While the restaurant is located uphill and is quite far from the busy city center, I can honestly say it is well worth the travel. The drive up the winding roads sets the stage for what turns out to be a spectacular evening.

My favorite part of the experience has to be the view. I absolutely love the scenery during the night; seeing the entire city lights twinkling below while you enjoy the cool mountain breeze is breathtaking. Because it is such a popular spot, the restaurant is often very busy, creating a lively and energetic atmosphere. Despite the crowd, the service remains attentive and the food is delicious. They offer a great variety of dishes that highlight local flavors with a modern twist. Every meal we ordered was served fresh and full of flavor, making the long trip feel like a small price to pay for such high-quality dining.

Pros & Cons
Pros:
• Stunning Night View: Offers one of the best panoramic views of Cebu City and the coastline.
• Delicious Food: A wide menu featuring tasty Filipino favorites and international fusion.
• Refreshing Ambiance: The open-air setup and cooler mountain air provide a perfect escape from the city heat.
Cons:
• Distance: It is a long, uphill drive, so you definitely need a reliable vehicle or a booked ride.
• Always Busy: Expect a wait time during peak hours and weekends because of its popularity. || author=Taurus Mom; rating=5; time=2025-11-01T03:36:24.742721754Z; relative=5 months ago; text=Hands down, this place is our family's go-to spot! They're always warm and accommodating, no matter how many kids are running around. We feel right at home every time we walk in. Highly recommend for families - we'll be back for sure! 5 stars isn't enough 😊👍

Special thanks to Leah 😊

name: CCLEX - Cebu City Point
address: 7WG7+9H, Lapu-Lapu, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Sir Chad; rating=5; time=2026-01-08T01:44:42.604463894Z; relative=3 months ago; text=CCLEX is an impressive and modern bridge that really showcases Cebu’s progress in infrastructure. The drive is smooth, the views of the sea especially during sunrise or sunset are beautiful, and it definitely makes travel between Cebu City and Cordova faster and more convenient.

However, near the SM Seaside area, particularly close to the port, there’s a noticeable amount of trash along the shoreline. It slightly takes away from the overall experience and the otherwise stunning view.

Overall, CCLEX is still a great landmark and a must-see when in Cebu. Hopefully, better waste management and cleanup in that area will make the experience even better in the future. || author=Mary Anne Cabo; rating=4; time=2025-10-23T03:21:31.939576176Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Even just a one-way jog on CCLEX is already a solid workout! The bridge is long, slightly inclined, and gives you amazing sea and skyline views — perfect for both casual walkers and serious runners. It can get quite hot if you go later in the morning, so it’s best to start early. Make sure to bring your own water or buy one before entering. Also, once you’re inside, it’s strictly one-way — you can’t turn back unless it’s an emergency. It does get a bit crowded on weekends and holidays, but other than that, it’s one of the best spots in Cebu for a scenic jog, walk, or just to enjoy the view and fresh air.

Tip: On our third visit, instead of heading back right away or grabbing breakfast at the nearby Jollibee, we jogged straight through the bypass road across (another 4KM) and took an e-trike to Plantation Bay for their breakfast buffet. || author=Gorj Supleo; rating=5; time=2025-09-28T15:20:49.051318585Z; relative=7 months ago; text=The Cebu-Cordova Link Expressway (CCLEX), also known as CCLEX - Cebu City Point, is an 8.9-kilometer-long cable-stayed bridge that connects Cebu City on mainland Cebu to Cordova town on Mactan Island, serving as the longest bridge in the Philippines. It was inaugurated on April 27, 2022, and is designed to ease congestion in Metro Cebu by providing a faster, more direct route to Mactan-Cebu International Airport, reducing travel time by approximately 40 minutes.
Been here September 18, 2025. || author=Jennifer Sardoncillo; rating=5; time=2025-09-17T23:20:05.923473819Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Great for joggers. Must prepare if you want to walk here since there’s no turning back once you started.

Coming from a break up, this is a fresh start for me. Recommended if you were heartbroken bc it will give you the will to move forward from life. || author=littleloise; rating=5; time=2026-02-12T03:14:43.916578901Z; relative=2 months ago; text=we passed under cclex while riding cokaliong from dumaguete to cebu. the sunrise by the bridge was absolutely stunning! 🌅✨

🗓️ september 10, 2025

name: Mount Mauyog Pinnacle Karst
address: FR6H+7GG, Balamban, Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Robert; rating=5; time=2025-08-09T10:42:36.899361955Z; relative=8 months ago; text=It's not easy climbing up there don't let these people fool you. Around 45 mins of very steep climbing to reach the top. At the top of this hill is the start of the trail and from there you can see the peak which is very high up and therefore gives you an idea of the height you need to climb. Still very much worth it but you just be careful. The final ascent is the hardest when you reach the top and want to make you way onto the rocks. || author=Mark Gigataras; rating=5; time=2025-02-12T02:36:24.029816Z; relative=a year ago; text=A short but moderately difficult climb up to this peak. Be ready to use your hands and feet as you go up these few meters to the top. Bring a walking stick to help with balance on your way down the slope. Mud and roots make the trail a little difficult to navigate, just do it one step at a time. Great views await you at the top. || author=Dan Paqueo; rating=5; time=2023-07-02T07:45:36.748430Z; relative=2 years ago; text=My recent climb with Mt. Mauyog this June 12, 2023 was truly very challenging for me.  It was a short minor climb. Rugged Terrain was very steep involving deep cliffs, sharp rocks/boulders, tall grasses and dark forest. The ascent was too abrupt and too much for me. From base to peak it's only less than 500m trekking,  but the level of difficulty is very high.

Now after 2days my legs are bearing all the pain from that climb.  I'm having a hard time walking.  Had i known beforehand that the trail was technically difficult for beginners like me that  requires physical and mental preparation i would not have climbed it that spontaneously.

And so i have great respect to all Climbers who have successfully reached the summits during every climb. I now truly understand what it takes to be a seasoned one. Cheers to us all 😊🙏👪💕👍🐦🐄🐜🐝🐞🍻🌲🌸🌳🍂🌾🍁🌿☀✨🌙🏁🌎 || author=Stevensonnet Art; rating=5; time=2023-06-14T01:05:39.374843Z; relative=2 years ago; text=Mount Mauyog Pinnacle Karst is not only a paradise for nature lovers but also a historical site of significance. As I ventured through the trail with my climbing enthusiast friends, we were captivated by the magnificent limestone formations and breathtaking views. Little did I know that this mountain holds the crash site of the late President Magsaysay, adding a profound sense of history to our journey. Don't miss the chance to explore this remarkable destination that combines adventure, natural beauty, and a glimpse into the past. It's an experience you won't forget! || author=Awesome Blessings; rating=5; time=2023-04-22T00:53:28.655405Z; relative=3 years ago; text=Great hike and magnificent views. We had 2 guides and well worth it. After the hike the local people were very welcoming. Definitely go back and hilke and hang with them again.

name: Lugsangan Peak
