sample_reviews: author=Anna Edoloverio; rating=5; time=2025-09-05T06:20:12.179396426Z; relative=7 months ago; text=It was quite an exciting hike from Osmeña Peak to Lugsangan Peak. Entrance fee is 50php, you can get a guide but it’s optional. We chose to go on our own since we’ve been hiking for quite a while. As long as you can manage but be careful not fall. The view is amazingly beautiful. || author=Elena Bujanovic; rating=5; time=2025-11-17T15:07:18.835180934Z; relative=5 months ago; text=From Osmẽna Peak to Lugsangan Peak, little bit more difficult hike but still not hard. || author=JG Travels; rating=5; time=2022-09-19T03:04:27.098427Z; relative=3 years ago; text=The trail going up is a bit challenging, but the 360° view at the peak is unbeatable. Tour guide is optional. Environmental fee ₱50 only. Hiking going up is around 10-15 minutes but the path is rocky and steep. || author=LeyMon; rating=5; time=2022-10-05T07:09:02.601058Z; relative=3 years ago; text=Trail is way more challenging compared to Osmeña Peak but it is shorter yet narrow. It was rainy when we went there and the view is 💯. Highly recommend to take a Osmeña Peak first before Lugsangan Peak so that you can compare. Panoramic bunch of hills will be your view. || author=Ken Michael Taban-ud; rating=5; time=2023-05-20T10:50:07.001795Z; relative=2 years ago; text=First Climb on this Peak was great as part of my Afternoon Run. It was kinda raining and foggy during the Climb. My observed conditions are that its kinda more challenging than Osmena peak. There are areas that one slip could lead to due to sudden fall but just be cautious you'll be fine. During my climb, it was having drizzle so the limestone rocks made it slippery and most of the trail have sharp rocks as opposed to Osmena peak so a little bit of care is needed and please don't climb Drunk it will be ⚕️. Slippers and casual wear is doable but not advisable though. Hiking shoes or anything with Hard Grippy Outsoles to protect your feet against hard rocks will be fine and make your climb comfy.

sample_reviews:
author=Paulo Niño; rating=5; time=2024-06-16T03:06:49.740494Z; relative=a year ago; text=Sacred Heart Church in Cebu is a serene and beautiful place of worship. Its architecture blends modern and traditional elements, creating a peaceful ambiance. The interior is spacious, fully air-conditioned, and well-maintained, with stunning stained glass windows and a reverent atmosphere. The community is warm and welcoming, making visitors feel at home. The church is conveniently located, and services are conducted in a solemn and organized manner. Overall, it’s a spiritually uplifting place, perfect for reflection and prayer. || author=AJQ; rating=5; time=2019-09-21T09:44:36.070986Z; relative=6 years ago; text=After it’s renovation, this church has transformed itself. No parish office during Monday but for those in Pilgrimage, someone can still stamp your passports. || author=MR Dy; rating=5; time=2018-01-09T07:32:16.572Z; relative=8 years ago; text=My family's church for three generations. Simple minimalist design. Good parking space. Serene.

name: Archdiocesan Shrine of Our Lady of Guadalupe
address: 969 V Rama Ave, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=cwade2009; rating=5; time=2025-12-16T07:10:11.093765843Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Tried to find the Starbuks head trail to no avail but explored this church instead. 20₱ parking. || author=Kim Alforque; rating=5; time=2024-12-17T03:24:09.501456Z; relative=a year ago; text=A must visit if you are into Churches and a devotee of Mama Mary.
The Church itself is Majestic!
It’s so solemn that it feels heart warming being inside the Church. I visited last Sunday after Our Lady of Guadalupe’s Feast day last December 12th.
They have a veneration area on the left side of the Church where you can kneel and pray to Our Lady and they also have a blessed Sacrament. || author=MENSEUNG; rating=5; time=2023-11-04T11:45:12.137028Z; relative=2 years ago; text=My recent visit to the Guadalupe church was a truly memorable experience. Its serenity, solemnity, and peaceful atmosphere make it a desirable place to worship. The interior is well maintained and orderly, featuring beautiful and ornate woodwork, which gives the building a majestic and dignified air to it. The organ is well kept and projects a wonderful reverberating sound throughout the chapel. The pews are evenly spaced, allowing everyone to have ample room to comfortably partake in the service. During our time there, the members were friendly and welcoming, which created a pleasant and warm atmosphere. I find the church is a great place to not only pray,  worship, and learn about the faith but also to take time away to relax. || author=Earl Darwin Chester Cabiling; rating=5; time=2023-03-04T23:19:25.704909Z; relative=3 years ago; text=The church is huge and looks amazing. I joined the 5:15 AM mass and people sang with the choir which gave me goosebumps. The outside of the church also looks historic with all the statues of saints and priests. || author=Diana Mellado (Miss President); rating=5; time=2024-11-20T21:09:36.092944Z; relative=a year ago; text=A very solemn place to pray and recollect for yourself. The place is very serene, especially on weekdays. It is located in Guadalupe Proper, Cebu City thus making it easy to commute via traditional jeep or taxi.

name: Archdiocesan Shrine of Our Lady of Lourdes
address: 7VVC+735, 430 Francisco Llamas St, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Caius; rating=5; time=2025-10-29T20:46:09.426015159Z; relative=6 months ago; text=The Archdiocesan Shrine of Our Lady of Lourdes has its own unique architecture. You can see its huge curve-shaped roof made of concrete and a combination blue-white paints reflecting the color of their patron saint. It is a cross shaped in birds eye view, has a grotto in front, and also a large tv screen inside for live streaming. The first I was there for sunday mass, it has a huge numbers of manimbahay. Perfect for praying to our Lord GOD and also for self reflection. || author=Enteng Nabablit; rating=5; time=2025-07-15T03:05:04.693324328Z; relative=9 months ago; text=This church is a peaceful and holy place. It feels calm and quiet, perfect for praying or just thinking. You can really feel God’s presence here. If you’re looking for a place to rest your heart and mind, this is a good spot to visit. || author=Rabbi Morata; rating=4; time=2023-02-16T01:42:13.206234Z; relative=3 years ago; text=Very solemn place & well-maintained, family - oriented & one can really be encouraged to pray & make one's devotions /prayers. Adoration Chapel is also just as solemn. The pilgrim site of our Lady of Lourdes is also clean & expansive (the whole Archdiocesan Shrine in general that is..)
There's also convenient places to eat nearby like LA Fortuna bakery...
So it's good to give a very good amount of donation to the Church/shrine since one can see where it goes... || author=Hyacinth Fidelis Joaquin; rating=5; time=2022-06-23T05:24:00.292108Z; relative=3 years ago; text=A beautiful shrine of Our Lady of Lourdes. It is by Gods Grace that pursuing up the path/ career i onced prayed for led me to places i would never think i would be able to do in this lifetime ie like seeing the Pope and going to Rome. I would love to go back to Cebu and visit this church again

name: Our Mother of Perpetual Help - Redemptorist Church
types: tourist_attraction, church, place_of_worship, association_or_organization, point_of_interest, establishment
address: 8V7W+FHP, Queen's Rd, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
description: Catholic masses & choral singing at a towering church with a striking, art deco-influenced design.
sample_reviews:
author=Ryan Patrick Cejudo; rating=5; time=2026-02-27T12:24:34.591253875Z; relative=a month ago; text=There’s a certain kind of peace I only find at Redemptorist Church, especially when I step inside away from the Mango Avenue traffic. It’s easily one of the most iconic spots in Cebu, and even if you aren’t there for the novena, the open-air architecture and the constant breeze through the side doors make it feel so welcoming. I love how the community here feels—from the candle sellers outside to the quiet hum of people praying at the shrine. Whether I’m attending a packed Wednesday mass or just sitting in a pew for ten minutes of silence, the atmosphere is always incredibly grounding and soulful. || author=Paulo Niño; rating=5; time=2024-06-11T11:13:56.959648Z; relative=a year ago; text=I attended a 5:30 pm Mass. The exterior of the church is currently being painted. Hopefully, the interior of the church will be painted too, as well as the tiles, since the church is located in the center of the metropolitan area. The renovation will enhance the church's appearance and create a more welcoming environment for parishioners and visitors alike. || author=Bethanne Cane; rating=5; time=2023-12-10T02:32:03.982642Z; relative=2 years ago; text=This place is a haven for Christians who have adoration for the Great Almighty and Mother Mary. They also have scheduled English mass for people who don't understand the local dialect Bisaya. One of the churches here I love to visit because of the solemnity and peaceful serenity of the location. My favorite place to contemplate life's journey and be thankful because life blessed us in any way. || author=Luchz P; rating=5; time=2025-03-25T10:33:59.938996Z; relative=a year ago; text=Breezy when the temperature 🌡️ is cool otherwise it's quite warm even though there are fans everywhere (most of it needs cleaning 😉).

Candle area - spacious and candle 🕯️ here are good (how it is made vs).

Choir/Songs: mm dunno why (not just here but in other Catholic Churches) they kept changing the melodies of the songs!
Back in the 90's it was lovely and people can song/hymn to it 😁

Audio: when you're seated in the front you can barely understand the Priest it sounds far.

Holy water 💦 blessing after the Mass depends on who presided. 🥹

As expected, it is crowded during Wednesday (Perpetual Help).. || author=Artemio III Masbate (Art); rating=5; time=2023-09-12T03:28:00.629584Z; relative=2 years ago; text=One of my favorite churches in Cebu. Parking is spacious so you don't have to worry about finding a spot. The audio system inside the church is superb. You get to understand every word said by the priest during the homily.

name: Monastery of the Holy Eucharist
types: tourist_attraction, church, place_of_worship, association_or_organization, point_of_interest, establishment
address: XJH2+PG6, Sibonga, Cebu, Philippines
description: Castlelike site where devotees leave offerings to the Virgin Mary in exchange for miracles.
sample_reviews:
author=cwade2009; rating=4; time=2025-12-09T00:04:01.629890535Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Dress code for entering inside but you can walk around outside and take pictures…parking is 20 pesos…beware of the scamming kids at the bottom of the hill who will tell you it’s the only place to park…drive all the way to the top and park your car in the proper place and avoid the walk uphill…it’s more secure…when I drove back down and they all saw me the scrambled and ran. Pretty awful to be using a religious site to scam people… || author=Dion McDonald; rating=5; time=2026-01-03T12:51:34.337812843Z; relative=3 months ago; text=This working monastery in Sibonga, Cebu is actually an incredible formation of multiple impressive buildings on a huge and spectacular area of land. Despite the hot day, huge crowds and long lines to enter, this was definitely an unmissable experience. Entry was free and although the grounds were vast, you could potentially complete a visit in as little as 60 mins. Touring all buildings and the grounds would take at least a number of hours however. Tip: visitors should wear modest, respectable clothing like long pants/skirts (below the knee). || author=Just Carter; rating=5; time=2025-10-31T02:25:07.473321162Z; relative=5 months ago; text=The Monastery of the Holy Eucharist, also known as Simala Shrine, is truly awe-inspiring place that blends deep spirituality with breathtaking architecture.  Nestled in the hills of Sibonga, it offers a serene atmosphere perfect for reflection and prayer.

The grandeur of its castle-like structure, the solemn silence, and the heartfelt devotion of pilgrims make it a sacred experience like no other.

Whether you come to pray, give thanks, or simply marvel at its beauty, Simala Shrine leaves you with peace, faith, and a sense of divine presence. || author=Joel Obenieta; rating=5; time=2025-12-13T03:44:30.109871572Z; relative=4 months ago; text=The Simala Church of Cebu, officially called the Monastery of the Holy Eucharist, is a famous Catholic pilgrimage site located in Sibonga, Cebu, Philippines.

It is best known for its miraculous image of the Virgin Mary (Our Lady of Lindogon). Many devotees visit the church to pray, give thanks, and ask for healing or miracles. The church is especially popular during Holy Week and Marian feast days.

Simala Church is also well known for its castle-like architecture, which resembles a European cathedral, and its scenic hilltop location. Visitors often describe it as both a spiritual and tourist destination, combining faith, prayer, and beautiful views. || author=Reymund Lara; rating=5; time=2026-02-20T13:12:47.900750049Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Simala Shrine in Cebu is a strikingly beautiful and serene destination. The atmosphere is deeply peaceful, making it an ideal place for prayer, reflection, and genuine spiritual connection with the Lord.

A holy sanctuary where faith and devotion are deeply felt. 🙏 Known as the patron saint of the souls in purgatory, San Nicolas continues to inspire the faithful with his intercession and miracles.

The classic architecture, solemn interiors, and enduring devotion of the faithful make it a must-visit for both pilgrims and travelers. 🌿 Whether for prayer, reflection, or simply to witness Cebu’s cultural faith tradition, this shrine offers a unique and moving experience.

sample_reviews: author=Paul Molina; rating=5; time=2024-04-09T05:55:09.379621Z; relative=2 years ago; text=A good mix of quality local and imported liturgical vessels, vestments, and the like. A good number of items suitable for use in the western-rite Orthodox tradition. The sisters are very patient and understanding and promise to be good conversationalists. I’ve had my cassocks and clerical shirts tailored by the elderly Sister Teresita whose hands remain steady and skillful. She will be missed when she returns to Luzon to take up a new assignment in Antipolo. Sister Anna (in photo) is a generous soul who goes out of the way to ensure you get what you need. As an Orthodox priest, the PDDM sisters bring to contemporary reality our history as sister churches by their way of biblical hospitality - even if their knowledge of the other ‘lung’ of the catholic church is lacking. sample_reviews:
author=Rob Mono; rating=1; time=2026-01-29T01:51:43.997046385Z; relative=2 months ago; text=If you’re a civilian clean people avoid this island!! Is the most horrible and polluted place in the planet .. traffic more intense than NY , Manila or Beijing , 24 on 24!!! also the sewer direct to the sidewalk, it’s dirty and full of rats jumping around! The only tourists  are the bag packers or the classic old mans with a young girl from here!! 6-7 millions of people living in a small island think about the mountains of trash and the smell !! || author=rune krumsvik; rating=5; time=2026-02-03T02:13:24.546832005Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Stayed 1 month in cebu,from January to February, the weather was quite nice, not too warm 25-30,clouded,so perfect for us from Europe, had two days in Oslob and Moalboal diving with the whalesharks and sardines and turtles,,the most fantastic days down here,just amazing, wouldn't miss that experience if you like nature,also tumalog waterfalls,,,but i recommend that you get a person from cebu too guide you, beacouse it can take very long waiting time,,I highly recommend Shierell Buenconsejo Ruela, just find her on Facebook,,the food down here is very internasjonal,and the local food is very good, pretty sheap prices compared too Europe, will surely be back 😀😀❤️ || author=Miky Valdez; rating=1; time=2026-02-05T10:08:09.941177003Z; relative=2 months ago; text=The island of the apocalypse.
We arrived with very different expectations: a peaceful island surrounded by beautiful sea. What we found was the complete opposite.
The beaches are very limited and mostly only in the far north and south. and not even all of them are worth visiting. That’s extremely disappointing, because when you go to an island, you don’t expect just a handful of decent beaches.
The traffic is absolutely insane. We were stuck for two hours in the city center without moving at all. The island feels dirty and heavily polluted. The chaos honestly reminded me of India or Cebu City’s center ,constant congestion, noise, and smog everywhere plus rats!!
Getting from Lapu-Lapu Airport to either the north or south of the island can take almost four hours for just 100 km. That alone says everything about how badly organized and overwhelmed this place is. || author=Pio Portanto; rating=1; time=2026-02-06T10:47:48.469547880Z; relative=2 months ago; text=The Nightmare Island
This place was a huge disappointment. There are basically only three things to do on the entire island. First, the whale shark experience,which is completely overcrowded. You’re surrounded by hundreds of people trying to do the same thing. After waiting for hours, you finally get into the water with about 20 people from your group, and the experience lasts only 20–30 minutes.
The other options are the waterfalls and Sumilon Island. Sumilon Island was packed with local tourists having barbecues, blasting loud music, and leaving the beach dirty.The waterfalls were similarly overcrowded and chaotic.And then there’s Cebu City itself. a complete nightmare. Endless traffic, cars and motorbikes driving with no apparent rules, and a city that feels dirty, noisy, and overwhelming.
Overall, chaotic, overcrowded, and not worth the hype. || author=Valentina; rating=1; time=2026-01-31T16:14:33.497746337Z; relative=2 months ago; text=The only island that feels like it has no sea. Aside from a few beaches near Oslob, there’s really nothing else. Roads and houses have been built without any planning.almost right up to the shoreline, leaving hardly any natural beach areas. The city center is chaotic at all hours, dirty, and heavily polluted. Overall, a very disappointing experience. OVERRATED FOR SURE !!!
